signals.title: AUD/USD — G10 Bilateral Theme + Geopolitical Shock
signals.text: AUD/USD at 0.6999 — USD outperformance (1W avg +0.886%) vs AUD laggard status (1W avg -1.300%) creates a critical 2.187% G10 spread. Middle East hostilities and the Strait of Hormuz disruption drive a risk-off flight to USD, while AUD is pressured by a negative CESI (net -8 misses) and a crowded COT LF net long (+56,800, 76.5% long). The bear steepener regime (2s10s +91.4bp) and VIX at 22.2 favor USD funding over high-beta AUD carry (-60bp OIS deficit). Implication: AUD/USD remains the primary G10 expression for geopolitical risk-off and commodity supply shocks.

signals.title: USD/JPY — Carry + Intervention Risk + Correlation Break
signals.text: USD/JPY at 160.51 — the pair holds above the 160.00 threshold despite previous BoJ verbal warnings, as the +290bp OIS carry advantage (Fed 3.75% vs BoJ 0.73%) dominates. A significant correlation break vs US 10Y (z=+1.73σ) suggests USD/JPY is ignoring yield stabilization in favor of safe-haven USD demand amid Middle East escalation. COT LF net short -105,136 (29.5% long) indicates a crowded carry trade vulnerable to BoJ intervention, though 25d RR at -1.19 shows the options market is already paying for downside protection. Implication: Sustained trade above 160.00 increases the probability of direct BoJ action.

signals.title: NZD/USD — COT vs Retail Divergence + Real Rate Gap
signals.text: NZD/USD at 0.5795 — extreme retail sentiment (88% long) diverges from institutional positioning (COT LF net short -27,797, 15.1% long). This institutional-retail gap occurs as NZD faces a deeply negative real policy rate of -0.83% vs USD +1.31%, a 214bp real yield disadvantage. With NZD/USD down -1.28% on the week and retail attempting to fade the move, the risk of a retail capitulation flush remains high. Implication: Institutional short bias is supported by the real rate differential; retail longs are likely to be squeezed if 0.5750 fails.

signals.title: GBP/USD — Volatility Premium + Options Skew
signals.text: GBP/USD at 1.3368 — IV at 11.0% vs HV30 at 6.4% generates a +4.6% Vol Risk Premium (VRP), signaling aggressive institutional hedging ahead of the June 18 BoE meeting. Despite a neutral nominal carry (0bp), the 25d RR at -0.81 (moderate put skew) aligns with the broader USD bid. GBP COT LF net long +27,022 (65.6% long) represents a positioning overhang that is likely to unwind if Middle East tensions continue to support the DXY. Implication: High VRP suggests the market is pricing a larger-than-realized move; downside protection is being actively bid.

signals.title: USD/CAD — Correlation Break + Retail Extreme
signals.text: USD/CAD at 1.3942 — the pair exhibits a correlation break vs WTI Oil (z=-1.71σ), as CAD fails to benefit from the +2.02% surge in crude prices. Retail sentiment is at an extreme 86% short, attempting to fade the 1M +1.92% rally. The +139bp OIS carry advantage (Fed 3.75% vs BoC 2.24%) and the BoC's signal of policy patience on June 10 support the USD/CAD bid regardless of energy prices. Implication: CAD's traditional link to oil is secondary to USD funding demand and carry; retail shorts face further squeeze risk toward 1.4000.
